premises.premise: $BaCu_{3}O_{4}$ has the lowest melting point among the Ba-Cu oxides. 12
premises.assumption: Melt-solid reactions take place more completely than typical solid-state reactions.
premises.premise: The onset temperature of a superconducting transition is estimated as the temperature at which the curvature in the resistance versus temperature plot changes most rapidly. 13

premise.title: Effect of Calcium substitution in Y- and La-based superconductors

premises.premise: A 50% substitution of calcium for barium in $YBa_{2}Cu_{3}O_{7-x}$ depresses the superconducting transition temperature, whereas a similar substitution in the $LaBa_{2}Cu_{3}O_{7-x}$ compound increases the superconducting transition temperature. 13

reasoning.step: The authors select $Tl_{2}O_{3}$, CaO, and $BaCu_{3}O_{4}$ as starting materials. $BaCu_{3}O_{4}$ is specifically chosen based on its properties of having the lowest melting point among Ba-Cu oxides [@premise-1] and high fluidity in the molten state [@premise-2], under the assumption that melt-solid reactions take place more completely than typical solid-state reactions [@assumption-3].

A sample with the nominal composition $Tl_{2}Ca_{1.5}BaCu_{3}O_{8.5+x}$ is prepared by mixing the starting materials, pressing them into a pellet, heating to 880–910 °C for 3–5 minutes in flowing oxygen, and subsequently furnace-cooling to room temperature.
The resistance of the sample is measured using the standard four-probe technique with silver paste contacts on a rectangular bar cut from the pellet.

The resistance versus temperature plot for the furnace-cooled $Tl_{2}Ca_{1.5}BaCu_{3}O_{8.5+x}$ sample (shown in Fig. 1) indicates that resistance starts to drop sharply at $\sim 120$ K and reaches zero at $\sim 100$ K.
From the resistance data, the authors determine the midpoint of the superconducting transition to be 112 K and the zero-resistance temperature to be 103 K.

The transition temperatures of the $Tl_{2}Ca_{1.5}BaCu_{3}O_{8.5+x}$ sample (112 K midpoint, 103 K zero resistance) are compared with a $Tl_{1.86}CaBaCu_{3}O_{7.8+x}$ sample annealed at 450 °C and a $EuBa_{2}Cu_{3}O_{7-x}$ sample. The authors observe that these values are the highest among the Tl–Ca/Ba–Cu–O samples prepared in the study and exceed those of high-quality rare-earth-containing samples.

inference_unit.conclusion: A furnace-cooled sample with the nominal composition $Tl_{2}Ca_{1.5}BaCu_{3}O_{8.5+x}$ exhibits a superconducting transition midpoint at 112 K and a zero-resistance temperature at 103 K, which are the highest values recorded among the prepared Tl–Ca/Ba–Cu–O samples.
